name: KamboConnect
user_description: Find and connect with verified Kambo practitioners worldwide. Our platform prioritizes safety, experience, and client reviews to help you discover the right practitioner for your healing journey.

pages.Education:
import React from "react"; import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"; import { Alert, AlertDescription } from "@/components/ui/alert"; import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; import { AlertTriangle, Shield, Heart, Brain, Leaf, CheckCircle, Clock, Users, BookMarked } from "lucide-react"; export default function Education() { return (

Learn more about Kambo

For Clients

( <>

Kambo is the name given to the waxy secretion of a giant green monkey frog, known by the scientific name Phyllomedusa bicolor. This frog is native to the upper Amazon basin and is found in parts of Brazil, Peru, Colombia, and other neighboring countries.

For centuries, indigenous tribes such as the Matsés, Katukina, and Yawanawá have used Kambo for its purported healing and cleansing properties. Traditionally, it was used to boost stamina and hunting skills, to cleanse the body of "panema" (bad luck or negative energy), and to treat various illnesses. The secretion is ethically harvested without harming the frog, which is then safely returned to its habitat.

)} /> ( <>

Participants in Kambo ceremonies often report a wide range of benefits, both immediate and long-term. The secretion contains a complex cocktail of bioactive peptides that have potent effects on the human body.

Commonly reported benefits include:

  • Deep physical and energetic cleansing.
  • Strengthened immune system.
  • Increased energy, stamina, and mental clarity.
  • Reduced inflammation and pain.
  • Relief from depression, anxiety, and PTSD.

Disclaimer: These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. Kambo is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.

)} />

For Practitioners

( <>

Serving Kambo is a sacred responsibility that requires comprehensive training. When seeking a course, look for programs that emphasize:

  • Lineage & Tradition: Respectful teaching of the traditional origins and uses of the medicine.
  • Safety First: In-depth training on contraindications, risk assessment, emergency response, and hygienic practices.
  • Hands-On Experience: Supervised, practical application and receiving of Kambo under the guidance of experienced teachers.
  • Integration Support: Education on how to help clients integrate their experience physically and emotionally.
  • Ethical Conduct: Strong focus on practitioner ethics, scope of practice, and creating a safe ceremonial space.
)} /> ( <>

A practitioner's primary duty is the safety of their clients. While this platform provides detailed information on contraindications, core protocols for every session must include:

  • Thorough Screening: Conducting a detailed health intake for every client, every time. Verbally re-confirming key contraindications before the session.
  • Informed Consent: Ensuring clients understand the process, risks, and what to expect, and have signed a waiver.
  • Hydration Management: Carefully monitoring a client's water intake before and during the ceremony to prevent hyponatremia.
  • Sterile Practices: Using clean, sterile materials for making gates and handling the medicine.
  • Vigilant Observation: Never leaving a client unattended during the process and being trained to recognize and respond to adverse reactions.
)} /> ( <>

As part of the KamboConnect community, practitioners are expected to adhere to the highest ethical standards. These include:

  • Integrity: Representing your training, experience, and the medicine itself with honesty and humility.
  • Respect: Honoring the indigenous origins of Kambo and the sacred nature of the frog.
  • Confidentiality: Protecting client privacy and personal information.
  • Right Relationship: Maintaining clear and professional boundaries with clients. Never making unsubstantiated healing claims.
  • Collaboration: Fostering a supportive, non-competitive community with other practitioners, focused on collective well-being and safety.
)} />
{/* Safety Warning */} Important: Kambo should only be administered by trained and experienced practitioners. This information is for educational purposes only and does not replace professional medical advice.
{/* What is Kambo */} What is Kambo?

Kambo is a traditional Amazonian medicine derived from the waxy secretion of the Phyllomedusa bicolor frog (Giant Monkey Frog). Indigenous tribes have used it for centuries for physical and spiritual cleansing.

The secretion contains bioactive peptides that can have various effects on the body, including purging, increased heart rate, and potential therapeutic benefits.

{/* How it Works */} How Does Kambo Work?

Kambo is applied to small burns (gates) created on the skin. The secretion enters the lymphatic system and triggers various physiological responses.

Activates the immune system
Stimulates natural detoxification
May influence neurotransmitter balance
{/* Safety Guidelines */} Critical Safety Guidelines {/* Contraindications Warning */}

⚠️ Kambo Contraindications

These conditions, if present, indicate that Kambo should not be administered. Administration under any of these circumstances may pose serious risk to health and safety.

{/* Absolute Contraindications */}

🔴 Absolute Contraindications

(Kambo should never be administered under these conditions)

  • Pregnancy (at any stage)
  • Breastfeeding (until complete cessation)
  • Heart conditions, including:
    • • Enlarged heart
    • • Irregular heartbeat (arrhythmias)
    • • Congestive heart failure
    • • Implanted pacemakers or defibrillators
    • • Heart valve replacements
    • • History of heart attack or stroke
  • Aneurysms (or family history)
  • Blood clots, thrombosis, or embolism history
  • Stroke or brain hemorrhage (history of)
  • Severe or active psychiatric disorders:
    • • Schizophrenia
    • • Bipolar disorder (unmedicated or unstable)
    • • Psychosis (current or past)
  • Organ transplant recipients
  • Addison's Disease
  • Recent surgery (within 6–8 weeks)
  • Recent COVID-19 vaccine (within the last 3 months)
  • Chronic low blood pressure (hypotension) that is symptomatic
  • High blood pressure – must be stable and well-controlled; monitor during session
  • Active infection or illness (especially flu, COVID-19, or fever)
  • Epilepsy or seizure disorders (unless well-managed with practitioner approval)
  • Serious autoimmune conditions (e.g., Lupus, MS – use with great caution)
  • Severe asthma or other chronic lung conditions
  • Current chemotherapy or radiation treatment
  • Severe liver or kidney impairment
  • Use of recreational drugs, alcohol, or entactogens within the last 48–72 hours
  • Current psychiatric medications:
    • • SSRIs (e.g., Prozac, Zoloft)
    • • MAOIs
    • • Antipsychotics
    • • Benzodiazepines
    • • Lithium
{/* Relative Contraindications */}

🟠 Relative Contraindications / Use With Caution

(Kambo may be used if the condition is managed and practitioner is experienced)

  • Diabetes (Type I or II) – monitor blood sugar levels, avoid during fasting or dehydration
  • Asthma – must have inhaler on site and be under control
  • Migraines – if active, postpone session
  • Thyroid disorders – especially if taking medications
  • Severe anemia
  • Elderly (70+) – only with full medical clearance
  • Minors (under 18) – generally contraindicated except in specific cultural/traditional contexts
  • Immunocompromised individuals
{/* Temporary Contraindications */}

🟡 Temporary Contraindications

(Wait appropriate time before administering Kambo)

  • COVID-19 infection – wait minimum 6 weeks after full recovery
  • COVID-19 vaccine – wait at least 3 months post-vaccine to ensure no interaction with immune system
  • Fasting for more than 24 hours – can cause blood pressure drop
  • Recent use of Bufo (5-MeO-DMT) – wait 2–4 weeks minimum
  • Iboga/Ibogaine – wait at least 3 months
  • Ayahuasca/Yagé – wait at least 7–10 days
  • Intense emotional trauma or recent loss – practitioner discretion recommended
{/* This column previously contained the brief contraindications, which are now replaced by the comprehensive block above. */} {/* Only Safety Requirements are kept here */}

Safety Requirements

  • Work only with verified, experienced practitioners
  • Complete thorough health screening
  • Ensure clean, sterile environment
  • Have medical support available if needed
  • Follow all preparation guidelines
  • Plan for proper aftercare and integration
{/* What to Expect */}
What to Expect

During the Session:

  • • Intense physical sensations
  • • Nausea and possible purging
  • • Increased heart rate and blood pressure
  • • Facial swelling (temporary)
  • • Strong energy followed by weakness

Duration:

Acute effects typically last 20-40 minutes, with full recovery within 1-2 hours.

Choosing a Practitioner
Essential Proper training and certification
Important Years of experience with Kambo
Critical Clear safety protocols in place
Valuable Positive client testimonials

Always trust your intuition and take time to research and interview potential practitioners before committing to a session.

{/* Final Notice */} Remember: Your safety and well-being are paramount. Take time to research, ask questions, and ensure you feel completely comfortable with your chosen practitioner and setting before proceeding with any Kambo ceremony.

pages.Disclaimer:
import React from "react"; import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"; import { Alert, AlertDescription } from "@/components/ui/alert"; import { AlertTriangle, Shield, FileText } from "lucide-react"; export default function Disclaimer() { return (

Legal Disclaimer

Important legal information and limitations of liability

{/* Main Warning */} Important: Please read this disclaimer carefully before using this platform. By using the Kambo App, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to these terms. DISCLAIMER & LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

The information and services presented on the Kambo App are intended solely for educational, cultural, and informational purposes. This platform facilitates connection between independent Kambo practitioners and clients but does not employ, endorse, supervise, or assume responsibility for any practitioner, their conduct, or the outcome of any session.

Kambo is a traditional shamanic ritual originating from Indigenous Amazonian communities. It is not a medical treatment and is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or medical condition. Statements regarding potential health benefits of Kambo have not been evaluated by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

The Kambo App does not provide medical or psychological advice, nor does it act as a healthcare provider or practitioner. No content or connection made through this platform shall be construed as medical, therapeutic, or clinical guidance. Users are strongly advised to consult a licensed physician or mental health professional regarding any health-related conditions or concerns prior to engaging in any practices discussed or facilitated through this platform.

All practitioners listed on the app are independent entities, and any agreements or interactions with them are entered into at the user's own discretion and risk. Users are solely responsible for conducting their own due diligence. The Kambo App makes no warranties, representations, or guarantees regarding practitioner competence, training, safety practices, or legal compliance.

USE OF THIS APP IS AT YOUR OWN RISK.

In no event shall the Kambo App, its creators, affiliates, licensors, or administrators be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of or relating to your use of this platform or engagement with any practitioner listed herein.

By accessing and using this app, you acknowledge and agree to assume full responsibility for any personal decisions, risks, or outcomes arising from your participation in any ceremonies or interactions related to Kambo. You further agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Kambo App and its affiliates from any claims, damages, or liabilities, including legal fees, arising from your use of the platform.

Testimonials and user experiences shared on the app are individual and anecdotal in nature and should not be interpreted as typical, scientific, or universally applicable. No results are guaranteed or implied.

Medical Advice Disclaimer

ALWAYS SEEK THE ADVICE OF A QUALIFIED PHYSICIAN OR HEALTHCARE PROVIDER with any questions you may have regarding a medical or psychological condition. Never disregard medical advice or delay seeking it because of information accessed through this app.

Last updated: {new Date().toLocaleDateString()}

); }

components.ui/slider:
import * as React from "react"
import * as SliderPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-slider"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Slider = React.forwardRef(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <SliderPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("relative flex w-full touch-none select-none items-center", className)}
    {...props}>
    <SliderPrimitive.Track
      className="relative h-1.5 w-full grow overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-primary/20">
      <SliderPrimitive.Range className="absolute h-full bg-primary" />
    </SliderPrimitive.Track>
    <SliderPrimitive.Thumb
      className="block h-4 w-4 rounded-full border border-primary/50 bg-background shadow transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50" />
  </SliderPrimitive.Root>
))
Slider.displayName = SliderPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Slider }
components.practitioner/ApplicationSuccess:
import React from "react"; import { Card, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card"; import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; import { Alert, AlertDescription } from "@/components/ui/alert"; import { CheckCircle, Clock, Mail, FileText, Users, Shield } from "lucide-react"; import { createPageUrl } from "@/utils"; import { Link } from "react-router-dom"; export default function ApplicationSuccess({ practitioner }) { return (

Application Submitted Successfully!

Thank you for applying to become a verified practitioner on KamboConnect. Your application is now under review.

Confirmation Email Sent: We've sent a confirmation email to {practitioner.email} with your application details and next steps.

What Happens Next?

1

Initial Review (1-2 days)

Our team reviews your application and basic credentials.

2

Verification Process (2-3 days)

Background check, training verification, and safety protocol review.

3

Final Approval & Activation

Profile activation and access to practitioner dashboard.

Once Approved, You'll Get Access To:

Full Practitioner Dashboard

Manage bookings, messages, and profile

Event Creation

Host circles, workshops, and retreats

Verified Badge

Show clients you're verified and trusted

Client Communication

Direct messaging and booking management

Have questions about your application? We're here to help.

); }

is_verified.description: Whether the practitioner has been verified

rejection_reason.description: Reason for application rejection, if applicable

listing_tier.description: The monetization listing tier for the practitioner

video_interview_url.description: URL for an embedded video interview (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo)

profile_image_url.description: URL for the practitioner's profile picture

image_urls.description: Array of additional images (ceremonies, space, etc.)

safety_protocols.description: Description of safety measures and protocols

cpr_certification_url.description: URL to uploaded CPR certification document

kambo_certification_url.description: URL to uploaded Kambo certification document

practitioner_id.description: ID of the practitioner being reviewed

booking_id.description: ID of the completed booking this review is for

reviewer_name.description: Name of the person leaving the review

reviewer_id.description: ID of the user leaving the review

would_recommend.description: Whether the reviewer would recommend this practitioner

verified_client.description: Whether this is a verified client review (true if linked to a booking)

practitioner_id.description: ID of the practitioner hosting the event

location_details.description: Additional details about the location (e.g., 'Studio A', 'Main entrance')

previous_kambo_experience.description: Whether the participant has previous Kambo experience

special_requirements.description: Any special requirements or dietary restrictions

title.description: The title of the forum post

content.description: The main content of the post, can be HTML

author_id.description: The ID of the user who created the post

author_name.description: The name of the user who created the post

post_id.description: The ID of the post this reply belongs to

content.description: The content of the reply, can be HTML

author_id.description: The ID of the user who wrote the reply

author_name.description: The name of the user who wrote the reply

practitioner_id.description: ID of the practitioner being booked

client_id.description: ID of the user making the booking, if logged in

client_name.description: Name of the client making the request

requested_date.description: Client's preferred date for the session

message.description: A message from the client to the practitioner

conversation_id.description: ID of the conversation this message belongs to

booking_id.description: ID of the booking this message relates to (if applicable)

sender_id.description: ID of the user sending the message

receiver_id.description: ID of the user receiving the message

file_url.description: URL to attached file if message_type is file

event_id.description: ID of the event being paid for (if applicable)

booking_id.description: ID of the booking this payment relates to (if applicable)

user_id.description: ID of the user making payment

practitioner_id.description: ID of the practitioner receiving payment (if applicable)

related_booking_id.description: Associated booking if conversation started from booking

user_id.description: ID of the user receiving the notification

related_id.description: ID of related entity (booking_id, message_id, event_id, etc.)

action_url.description: URL to navigate to when notification is clicked

sender_image_url.description: URL of the sender's profile image for display

metadata.description: Additional data specific to notification type

user_id.description: ID of the user who favorited the item

item_id.description: ID of the favorited item (practitioner_id, event_id, post_id)

item_title.description: Title/name of the favorited item for display

metadata.description: Additional data about the favorited item

date.description: The blocked date in YYYY-MM-DD format

start_time.description: Start time in HH:MM format (optional - if not provided, blocks entire day)

end_time.description: End time in HH:MM format (optional - if not provided, blocks entire day)

block_type.description: Type of block - full day, partial day, or exception to regular availability

reason.description: Optional reason for blocking (vacation, personal, etc.)

external_calendar_event_id.description: ID from external calendar system (Google Calendar, etc.)

date.description: The specific date for this exception

day_of_week.description: Day of week this exception applies to

exception_type.description: Override normal hours, add extra availability, or remove availability

start_time.description: Exception start time in HH:MM format

end_time.description: Exception end time in HH:MM format

reporter_id.description: ID of the user making the report

resolution_action.description: Action taken to resolve the report

profile_image_url.description: URL to the user's profile image

role.description: The role of the user in the app

full_name.description: The full name of the user

profile_image_url.description: URL to the user's profile image
